mentions.note: IPO outsource manufacturer for aerospace/defense; uniquely positioned at day-one-to-full-scale production. Supports both legacy primes and SpaceX/Blue Origin. Decision velocity (3–5yr vs. 10–20yr programs) and IP-enabled manufacturing enable fast response to urgent drone/missile/satellite demand post-Iran restocking. CEO Trip Ferguson projects urgency on generational geopolitical demand. Cramer: rare IPO that gets discovered over time with strong fundamentals; will outperform hype-cycle plays.
Hung in there despite scuttlebutt on app store slowdown (typically analyst pretext). Not majorly hurt or helped by Samsung rotation; benefited from Mag 7 bounce but held up on its own merits.

Up $3.50 despite analyst downgrade from hold to sell — bullish signal. Enterprise software benefiting from rotation away from hardware; recent analyst downside miss ignored as market reprices defensives.
Semi equipment maker; reported strong results but stock fell on narrative that hardware orders peaked and data center buildout slowing. Samsung miss triggered systematic repricing.

Hyperscaler receiving gigantic money inflows after weeks of zombie-like performance. $25B bond offering (no more deals in 2026) didn't hurt stock. Walking dead are walking again as data center demand reassessed.
Healthcare distributor up another $2 as part of defensive sector rotation into healthcare before earnings Thursday; investor flight from cyclical tech hardware.
Reported blowout earnings month ago. Cramer recommends disciplined 10-share accumulation strategy (buy 10 every 3 weeks vs. lump sum).
Heavy equipment supplier went through similar selloff as data center hardware plays; physical infrastructure demand concerns bleeding across industrial sector.
Clubbed on data center weakness spillover from Samsung miss. Part of hardware supply chain losing favor as investors rotate to software and hyperscalers.
Enterprise software with no component/chip shortage exposure. Benefited from remarkable rotation into defensive software before AI era; no shortages like hardware companies.

CEO Steve Angel (proven operator from Lindy) executing rail revival strategy. Stock at $48 has path to $60. Real-economy infrastructure play; strong long-term compounder if discipline persists in transportation sector.

Transitioning from cyclical boom-bust to secular growth winner via capacity discipline (no more aggressive expansion), premium focus, credit card partnerships (13% revenue). CEO/CFO signaling less oil-price hostage-taking. Up 28% YTD, fresh all-time highs. Multiple expansion thesis intact: currently 15x earnings vs. S&P 500 avg 22x; EPS growth $6 (2026) → $8.50 (2027) → $10 (2028). Buy on dips.

Secondary offering Friday larger than expected but oversubscribed. Fundamentals gorgeous despite share dilution. Cramer wouldn't mind owning for trust; dismiss short-term supply concerns and focus on business quality.
Hyperscaler getting renewed bid as investors rotate back into Mag 7 hyperscalers from commodity hardware. Money flowing in despite weeks of weakness.

CEO Jim Courier (hired by Dave Cody, legendary CEO) created pure-play aerospace company from diversified conglomerate. Aerospace and Defense segment is blue-chip beneficiary of defense spending cycle. Cramer keeping for trust, open to buying more; prefer over Boeing.
Healthcare defensive rotation: stock jumped nearly $8 ahead of earnings as money fled hardware/components into full-immunity healthcare plays amid uncertainty.
Semi equipment maker reported great results but stock collapsed on Samsung spillover. Investors suddenly worried about peaking semiconductor orders and slowing data center buildout.
Semiconductor equipment supplier caught in rotation away from components; recent positive guidance forgotten as investors extrapolate Samsung weakness to industry-wide demand decline.
Hyperscaler bouncing on rotation back to Mag 7 after hardware selloff. Defying potential lawsuit/tobacco-like liability concerns as sentiment shifts. Part of 'walking dead' that got fresh money.

Hallowed stock usually trades with Mag 7 but lately trading with defensive enterprise software. Cramer bullish on low prices as gift; trying to hold for trust despite prior self-flagellation. Stock benefiting from hardware-to-software rotation.

Reported incredible quarter but stock immediately sold off on Samsung's miss and broader hardware sector contagion. Investors fleeing entire semiconductor equipment chain despite company fundamentals.
Defensive enterprise software stock has 'gotten jiggy' (traded with Mag 7 before) — now seeing renewed bid as money rotates from commodity hardware into software with pricing power.

Rallied despite neverending drumbeat of customers vowing to minimize Nvidia bills via custom chips. At one point down huge, recovered on broader data center re-pricing. Market reassessing whether chip commodity compression is overstated.
Defensive consumer staple gained $1.50 during hardware selloff as investors rotate into less cyclical, full-immunity sectors ahead of mixed economic backdrop.

7% dividend safe but growth problems persist. CGEN acquisition hasn't delivered; can't identify near-term earnings inflection despite historically strong profile. Down near 52-week lows. Cramer wishes he could be more positive but lacks conviction on growth trajectory.

19 analyst initiation reports on IPO (biggest ever); 18 of 19 bullish, mostly clustered at $225 price target. Raymond James strong buy $800; Moffett Nathanson neutral at $131. Bull thesis: dominant rocket platform + Starlink + terrestrial AI + orbital compute = transformational. Moffett's clearest risk map: TAM skepticism, orbital economics, AI pricing durability, $2T valuation unjustifiable. Cramer bullish on long-term fundamentals but acknowledges execution risks on difficult milestones.

9% post-earnings drop triggered panic sector rotation away from hardware; up 370% in 12 months, 140% YTD — valuation unsustainable. Investors extrapolating Korean memory weakness to U.S. data center demand collapse.
Storage manufacturer hit alongside Western Digital as investors flee commodity hardware plays; extrapolating Samsung weakness to U.S. demand pessimism.
CEO trying turnaround; unclear if M&A catalyst will materialize. 4.75% yield supports hold. Wait for acquisition catalyst or strategic clarity.

Structural industry shifts toward brand loyalty over price-cutting; removing lower-margin capacity vs. slashing prices. CEO Kirby cites permanent, irreversible competitive changes. Up 15% YTD, fresh highs. Currently 13x earnings with EPS growing $10 (2026) → $15 (2027) → $17 (2028). Multiple expansion play if secular growth thesis holds. Buy into weakness.
Liked alongside CSX as transport theme. Railroad benefiting from discipline in infrastructure sector. Long rails and airlines as transport plays holding structural advantage.
Power and cooling equipment for data center; may be weaker despite historically strong performance. Caught up in sector-wide hardware selloff sparked by Samsung.
Storage equipment supplier caught in data center hardware rotation away from components. Club-like selling pressure across storage sector.

Down 18% from May $135 peak despite improving fundamentals (oil fell from $110 to low $70s, gas from $4.56 to $3.79). Q2 report fine; miss triggered by guidance reiteration vs. raise expectations and softer Q3. Trump price-cut announcement and tariff refund upside (0.5% of sales) not yet baked in. Buying opportunity as consumer headwinds ease.
